ksa_text: Ability to develop measurement and monitoring strategies, metrics, and procedures for tracking organizational compliance and risk status.

dcwf.top_element_label: Facilitate implementation of new or revised laws, regulations, executive orders, policies, standards, or procedures.

nice.top_element_label: Knowledge of Risk Adaptive (Adaptable) Access Controls (RAdAC)

nist_ai_rmf.top_element_label: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.

top_anchors.element_label: Facilitate implementation of new or revised laws, regulations, executive orders, policies, standards, or procedures.
Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.

pass_2_evaluation.rationale: Regulatory Compliance falls outside the primary competency scope of the VP Data Products. This ability KSA does not align with the role's core responsibilities and is excluded.

pass_3_adversarial_internal.ruling: No internal inconsistency identified. Domain scope, seniority fit, and signal level are internally consistent with exclusion.

pass_4_adversarial_external.challenge: Is Regulatory Compliance truly out-of-scope for the VP Data Products? Emerging role definitions may include broader competency expectations.
pass_4_adversarial_external.evidence: Current job market analysis and framework definitions (DCWF, DDAT, NICE) do not list Regulatory Compliance as a core or recommended competency for the VP Data Products. Role scope exclusion is consistent with external evidence.
pass_4_adversarial_external.ruling: Upheld: external evidence confirms Regulatory Compliance falls outside the established competency profile for the VP Data Products. Exclusion maintained.

decision_rationale: Regulatory Compliance is outside the competency scope of the VP Data Products. All evaluation passes consistently support exclusion.
